metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_5180
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 3: SynDIG1 interacts with GluA2 in heterologous cells.

Figure 4, 5: shRNA knockdown of SynDIG1 reduced the number of excitatory synapses in dissociated rat hippocampal neurons and decreased the frequency and amplitude of miniature excitatory postsynaptic current (mEPSC).

Figure 6, 7: Conversely, SynDIG1 overexpression promoted excitatory synapse development and increased mEPSC frequency and amplitude.

Figure 8: SynDIG1 distribution at excitatory synapses is activity regulated.
"Sodium channel-dependent action potentials in hippocampal neurons were blocked by addition of tetrodotoxin (TTX) at 10 DIV. Upon activity blockade for two to four days, SynDIG1 immunoreactivity redistributed from diffuse and punctate staining in dendrite shafts to bright clusters, presumably spines, protruding from dendrites (Figure 8A). The overall level of SynDIG1 protein did not change in neurons treated with TTX compared with vehicle as assessed by immunobloting with anti-SynDIG1 mAb (Figure S8A). Under control conditions, SynDIG1 is enriched 2.5-fold in spines relative to shafts; that enrichment increases significantly to 7.0-fold after TTX treatment (Figure 8B). In contrast, SynDIG1 puncta density in the presence or absence of TTX does not change (Figure 8C). Thus, SynDIG1 distribution but not synthesis is regulated by synaptic activity in hippocampal neurons.". Experimental description: M&M: "In dissociated rat hippocampal cultures, mEPSC recordings were performed by whole-cell patch-clamp technique"

constructs:
"SynDIG1 is annotated in the GenBank database as "tmem90b". Full length and truncated versions of mouse SynDIG1 coding sequence were amplified by PCR from the RIKEN AV149920 cDNA clone and inserted into pHM6 (Roche) to create an in-frame HA tag at the N-terminus. Constructs for SynDIG1 knockdown were created with the pSuper vector (Oligoengine) system to contain an 18-nucleotide target sequence (GCT GTG GCC AAA GGA GAC) or control sequence (GCT GTG GAC AAA GGA GAC) with a single nucleotide mismatch for the mouse and rat SynDIG1 genes."
